lesson.menu_title: I'll Handle It -- Instant Decisions and Offers with 'Will'
lesson.menu_desc: Offers, quick decisions and promises at an official dinner -- with 'will' -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- I'll Handle It -- Instant Decisions and Offers with 'Will'
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 7 -- Instant Decisions with 'Will'

listenings.text: Hello, Maria Clara. This is Daniel from the international office. I have a quick update for you. The rector of the Chinese delegation will arrive on Thursday, not on Friday. He has a small request: he would like a short meeting with you before the conference. In my opinion, it will be a very good opportunity. If you are available on Thursday afternoon, I will confirm the time immediately. Please reply when you can. Thank you, and have a good evening.

Good evening, everyone, and welcome to the official dinner. Here is the plan for tonight. In a few minutes, the waiters will serve the first course. After dinner, our host will make a short speech. Tomorrow, the conference will start at nine o'clock in the morning. If you have any request, our team is available at the front desk. We will not change the program tonight. Thank you very much, and please enjoy your evening.

extra_audio.text: Good evening. I am very happy to meet you. I'll have some water, please. And yes, I'll say a few words on Friday. I promise I won't be long.

items.situation: At the dinner, the waiter offers water or juice.
tips: A decision now = will: I'll have...

items.situation: The rector has a request: "Will you say a few words on Friday?"
tips: It depends on the schedule, but I'll do my best.
It depends on... + I'll do my best.

items.situation: You are a little worried about the time.
tips: Don't worry, I won't be late.
Negative promise = won't: I won't be late.

items.situation: A colleague asks: "Are you going to present tomorrow?"
tips: Yes, I am going to present in the morning.
A plan decided before = going to.

items.situation: Someone makes a proposal you like at the round table.
tips: I agree. I think it will be a great idea.
I agree + a prediction: it will be...

items.situation: An important email arrives and needs a fast answer.
